title: Arctanthemum kurilense
intro: Arctanthemum kurilense, also known as Korean chamomile or Kurile dandelion, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and promote relaxation, due to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Arctanthemum kurilense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Arctanthemum kurilense is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or extract, and is commonly used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Arctanthemum kurilense is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is classified in the genus Arctanthemum and the family Asteraceae, which are part of the larger kingdom Plantae. This plant is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, and family Asteraceae. The genus Arctanthemum is a group of plants closely related to the genus Anthemis. The classification of Arctanthemum kurilense is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
